behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-body/header handoff plus queue side effect, not just HMAC math. MOCK-MISS: this can fail when raw body bytes are transformed between HTTP layer and verifier. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP are not required. Rejected higher level: no behavior phrase requires real OS/network/subprocess; "signature does not match its body" is satisfiable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook handler with an in-process HTTP test harness and real middleware wiring for raw-body extraction + signature verification + queue adapter stub/spied module. Send a body with an intentionally wrong `X-Signature-256`; assert `401` and assert enqueue was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across handler and queue modules. MOCK-MISS: a pure unit test of one function can miss async ordering bugs at module boundary. REAL-INFRA not required by text; it does not require real network/subprocess, only sequencing. Rejected System due lack of phrase requiring real OS/network.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + real queue repository module (or lightweight local persistence implementation) in-process. Instrument queue `write` completion and response send time; assert `write` completes before `200` is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is module orchestration (ack path vs async worker path). A unit test of isolated logic can miss promise/await wiring errors across modules. No real OS/network requirement in phrase "without waiting for downstream processing"; can be proven in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process server wiring where downstream processor is a controllable promise that blocks. Send valid webhook and assert HTTP response returns before the processor promise resolves.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "within 3 seconds wall-clock time" and "slow and concurrent". REAL-INFRA: real timing, scheduler contention, and concurrent request handling can be masked by fake timers/in-process stubs. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing frequently passes while real event-loop/backpressure behavior violates SLA.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service process with real HTTP requests, generate concurrent load including earlier slow downstream jobs, measure end-to-end latency, and assert webhook responses stay under 3 seconds wall-clock.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at handler-to-persistent-queue state boundary. Unit-only logic can miss idempotency bugs caused by repository semantics. No phrase requires real OS/network/subprocess; "already received" can be reproduced with in-process persistent store fixture. Rejected System for lack of explicit real infra requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler + real dedup persistence layer (local DB/filesystem test instance). Submit same event ID twice; assert first write succeeds and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This validates error propagation/mapping across queue module and HTTP module. Behavior phrase "queue reports it is full" describes a module contract; no requirement to induce real infra saturation. Rejected System because behavior does not require real capacity exhaustion, only handling of reported-full condition.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue adapter that returns a typed `queue full` error. Assert response is `503` and includes `Retry-After` header with expected value/format.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is inside queue implementation + persistence wiring. Phrase "actually enforces its configured capacity limit" requires testing real queue storage behavior, not a mock. By level defs, local DB/filesystem-backed persistence is Integration. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external API/subprocess/OS primitive beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue implementation against a local test datastore/filesystem, set small capacity, enqueue up to limit, then assert next enqueue is rejected with the queue-full error.
